economic_notes: Nation is Host First Nation for River Cree Resort and Casino, receiving 30% of slot revenue via First Nations Development Fund. Operations include gas bar, convenience store, cannabis shop, tobacco shop, golf course, construction company, utilities, and real estate development. Age Care LP and Age Care GP Ltd incorporated to provide surgical facilities. Mamawatoskewak Trust holds power and utility project investment still in development stage.
notes: Complex consolidation with 31 fully consolidated subsidiaries and 12 entities on modified equity basis including River Cree Resort Casino entities. Long-term debt includes $199,128,941 owing to First Nations Finance Authority, of which $154,638,037 relates to Enoch Cree Nation Business Trust for casino-related financing. The nation advanced $155,000,005 to Enoch Cree Nation Business Trust during the year funded by FNFA borrowings. Yekau Lake specific claim settlement of $91,000,000 was received in prior year (2021) and placed in restricted trust. COVID-19 relief funding of $17,397,085 received from ISC included in revenue. Total government transfers calculated as ISC $32,340,454 + ISC-River Cree Resort $2,588,967 + CMHC $1,664,710 + ISETS $1,155,786 + Government of Alberta $447,140 + FNDF $16,971,998 + FNDF-River Cree Resort $11,499,750 = $66,668,805 less loss on River Cree investment $14,187,342 excluded as not a transfer; restricted cash includes Ottawa Trust $3,721,793, Yekau Lake Trust $55,859,100, Settlement Trust $6,913,071. Annual debt service calculated as LTD repayment $2,607,741 + FNFA repayment $1,229,227 = $3,836,968.
